output:
I’m reviewing the implementation for story `21-1-extract-workflow-actors` as a code review, focusing on correctness, security, architecture fit, and what can be verified against the story intent. I’ll inspect the changed code and any story artifacts first, then trace tests and edge cases before issuing a pass/fail verdict.I’ve confirmed the story is still in progress and the implementation is limited to `workflow-actors.ts` plus imports/exports in `workflow-machine.ts`. Next I’m reading the story spec and the actual diff side by side so I can judge architecture constraints and missed ACs, not just style.The story spec is much stricter than the current extraction: it requires a small actor-focused module with only workflow-type/external imports. I’m checking whether the current split preserved behavior, and whether the new module actually satisfies those layering constraints or just moved code around.The main risk is no longer behavioral breakage but a partial refactor that violates the story’s stated architecture. I’m pulling exact line references now so the review can be specific about what failed and what still needs proving.1. High: the extraction is incomplete, so the story’s main AC is not met. The story requires `workflow-actors.ts` to contain `dispatchActor`, `nullTaskActor`, `dispatchTaskCore`, and `nullTaskCore`, but `dispatchTaskCore` still lives in [workflow-machine.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-machine.ts#L237) and `dispatchActor` is still created there at [workflow-machine.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-machine.ts#L362). The new module explicitly documents this fallback at [workflow-actors.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-actors.ts#L184), which means the refactor stopped short of the intended boundary.

2. Medium: the new module violates the planned layering for `workflow-actors`. The story/architecture calls for an actor-focused module with only workflow-type and external dependencies, but [workflow-actors.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-actors.ts#L6) imports `output`, `agent-resolver`, `workflow-parser`, `null-task-registry`, `state`, `evaluator`, and `workflow-state`. That makes the file a mixed bag of engine types, prompts, coverage helpers, and null-task logic rather than the narrow actor module described in [epics-xstate-engine.md](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics-xstate-engine.md#L643) and [architecture-xstate-engine.md](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture-xstate-engine.md#L326).

3. Medium: AC coverage is weak because the new module is not independently tested. Existing tests still import the relevant APIs from [workflow-machine.test.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-machine.test.ts#L9) and [workflow-engine.test.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-engine.test.ts#L135), and there is no companion test file for [workflow-actors.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-actors.ts). I did run `npm run test:unit -- src/lib/__tests__/workflow-machine.test.ts`, which passed, so I do not see a direct behavioral regression from the import shuffle, but that does not prove the new actor module is independently testable as the story intends.

<verdict>fail</verdict>
<issues>
1. `dispatchTaskCore` and `dispatchActor` remain owned by `workflow-machine.ts`, so the actor extraction is incomplete and the primary story AC is not satisfied.
2. `workflow-actors.ts` breaks the intended architecture boundary by importing multiple runtime internals beyond workflow types/external dependencies.
3. No dedicated `workflow-actors` tests were added; current passing tests only cover the old `workflow-machine` surface, so AC coverage for “independently testable actors” is incomplete.
</issues>

Targeted verification: `npm run test:unit -- src/lib/__tests__/workflow-machine.test.ts` passed. I did not rerun the full unit/integration suite because the current repo already has unrelated failing checks from the prior verification context.
